title: 系列文章：保障 AI 技术栈安全：从模型到生产
author: Claudio Masolo
content:
AI has officially shifted from experimentation to production, outpacing legacy defenses and creating a volatile new security landscape. This challenge is defined by three critical frontiers: data poisoning, AI-driven phishing, and shadow cloud governance.

AI in production has fundamentally changed the security landscape. From the realistic deception of AI-driven phishing to the quiet corruption of poisoned datasets, these threats are systemic rather than isolated. Traditional controls are no longer enough; defenders must now assume that attackers are using the same sophisticated AI tools they are.

Securing AI requires rethinking security as a total lifecycle responsibility. This means protecting data integrity from ingestion to inference and baking governance into development pipelines. By aligning people, processes, and technology, organizations can ensure their AI is not only performant, but secure, transparent, and ready for the machine age.

The InfoQ "Securing the AI Stack: From Model to Production" article series provides your roadmap for the machine age, exploring how to move from vulnerable prototypes to resilient systems through layered defense, robust MLOps, and integrated governance. 

You can download the entire series collated in PDF format, in the associated eMag.

Series Contents

1

Artificial Intelligence-Driven Phishing: How Phishing Technique Is Evolving and Implemented

This article explains how AI has scaled phishing from manual tasks into high-velocity threats. By automating reconnaissance, generating realistic deepfakes, and optimizing delivery, AI enables even low-skilled actors to execute sophisticated social engineering. To remain resilient, modern defense strategies must now mirror these layered AI tactics to counter automated, personalized attacks. 

Article by: Marco Rizzi

To be released during the week of June 8, 2026

2

Governing AI in the Cloud: A Practical Guide for Architects

The article warns that "Shadow AI" and unregulated API calls have dangerously expanded organizational attack surfaces. To regain control, governance must be integrated into the delivery pipeline using model registries, automated security scanning, and unified observability dashboards.

Article by: Dave Ward

To be released during the week of June 15, 2026

3

Understanding ML Model Poisoning: How It Happens and How to Detect It

This article warns of the growing threat of training data manipulation, where subtle changes cause models to misbehave in unpredictable ways. From the corruption of Microsoft’s Tay chatbot to risks in medical diagnostic systems, these real-world incidents prove that securing data integrity from ingestion to inference is critical for long-term accuracy and safety.  

Article by: Igor Maljkovic

To be released during the week of June 22, 2026

4

Building Trust in AI: Security and Risks in Highly Regulated Industries

This article shows that while implementing robust MLOps practices for secure, scalable model management throughout their lifecycle, organizations must develop comprehensive responsible AI frameworks that prioritize fairness, transparency, ethical practices, and compliance with evolving regulations like GDPR and the EU AI Act. 

Article by: Stefania Chaplin, Azhir Mahmood

5

Security in the Machine Age: Expert Insights on AI Threat Evolution

This virtual panel, moderated by Claudio Masolo, underscores the need for security engineers to evolve alongside AI’s emergent behaviors. Panelists Elham Arshad, Sabri Allani, Vijay Dilwale, and Igor Maljkovic recommend specialized monitoring, novel forensic methodologies, and adaptive response frameworks to manage these unpredictable threats. 

Virtual panel by: Elham Arshad, Sabri Allani, Vijay Dilwale, Igor Maljkovic

To be released during the week of June 29, 2026
aiSummary: 本 InfoQ 系列文章为 AI 系统在生产环境中的安全提供了路线图，涵盖了 AI 驱动的网络钓鱼、影子云治理、模型投毒以及合规性要求。
oneSentenceSummary: 本 InfoQ 系列文章为 AI 系统在生产环境中的安全提供了路线图，涵盖了 AI 驱动的网络钓鱼、影子云治理、模型投毒以及合规性要求。
summary: InfoQ 的这系列文章概述了随着 AI 从实验阶段走向生产阶段所面临的关键安全挑战。文章指出了三个关键前沿领域：AI 驱动的网络钓鱼、数据/模型投毒以及影子云治理。该系列包括关于 AI 如何规模化网络钓鱼攻击的文章、一份关于在云中治理 AI 的实用指南、对 ML 模型投毒的分析、一篇关于在高监管行业中建立信任的专题文章，以及一场关于不断演变的 AI 威胁的虚拟小组讨论。其核心信息是，安全必须是一项贯穿整个生命周期的责任，需要将数据完整性、治理和稳健的 MLOps 从数据摄入到推理环节全面整合。

mainPoints.point: 生产环境中的 AI 引入了系统性的威胁，例如 AI 驱动的网络钓鱼和数据投毒，传统防御手段无法应对。
mainPoints.explanation: 该系列文章认为，AI 已将网络钓鱼从手动任务升级为自动化、高速度的攻击，同时被投毒的数据集可能导致模型行为不可预测，这要求从根本上重新思考安全问题。

mainPoints.point: 保障 AI 安全需要采用全生命周期的方法，将治理融入开发流程。
mainPoints.explanation: 组织必须保护从数据摄入到推理环节的数据完整性，使用模型注册表和自动化扫描，并协调人员、流程和技术，以确保 AI 的安全性和透明度。

mainPoints.point: 对于受监管行业的生产级 AI 而言，法规合规性（GDPR、欧盟 AI 法案）和负责任的 AI 框架至关重要。
mainPoints.explanation: 该系列文章强调，建立对 AI 的信任需要优先考虑公平性、透明度和道德实践的框架，同时结合稳健的 MLOps 来实现安全的模型管理。

keyQuotes: 保障 AI 安全需要将安全视为一项贯穿整个生命周期的责任。
传统的控制手段已不再足够；防御者现在必须假设攻击者正在使用与他们同样先进的 AI 工具。
